description: Trigger discrimination: third-party process-compliance audit vs writing your own closeout (ai-closeout-reporter), whole-repo health (full-codebase-auditor), diff correctness (code-reviewer), process design (ai-sdlc-operating-model), the forward ship/merge gate (release-readiness-reviewer), and codifying standing authority (agent-authorization-matrix).

cases.prompt: Before we widen the agent's autonomy, check whether its last five PRs actually followed our approval and validation discipline.
cases.reason: Third-party compliance verdicts on finished changes gate autonomy decisions — that is the governance audit, not self-reporting, not repo health, and not a forward release gate (the PRs already merged).

cases.prompt: You've just finished the refactor task — write up what you did, what you skipped, and what you validated.
cases.reason: First-person terminal disclosure of your own work is the closeout; the audit is the later third-party check of such reports.

cases.prompt: Here's the closeout report the agent produced for Phase 3 — verify its claims against what actually happened in the repo and PRs.
cases.reason: Cross-checking a closeout's claims against the primary record is the audit's C6 control, not writing another closeout.

cases.prompt: What's the overall state of this codebase — dead code, duplication, dependency risk, debt priorities?
cases.reason: Whole-repo code-state inventory is the codebase audit; no single change's process is in question.

cases.prompt: Review this PR's diff for bugs, missing tests, and security issues before we merge it.
cases.reason: Judging the code itself pre-merge is review; the governance audit checks after the fact that such a review HAPPENED.

cases.prompt: PR #52 is approved and we want to ship today — is it safe to merge and deploy? Check CI on the release commit, migration risk, rollback path, and flag defaults before we press the button.
cases.reason: A forward ship/merge-readiness gate on an upcoming release is release-readiness-reviewer; the governance audit is the retrospective did-it-follow-process verdict on changes already made.

cases.prompt: Last night a PR merged to main with zero human review because auto-merge fired. Audit what actually happened — who armed it, what approvals existed, and which controls failed.
cases.reason: Establishing what happened after an autonomy incident is the governance audit; the authorization matrix is the follow-up that codifies the corrected authority rule.

cases.prompt: After last week's auto-merge incident, write the standing rule: agents may open PRs, but arming auto-merge and merging to main always require a named human decision.
cases.reason: Codifying who holds merge/deploy authority going forward is the authorization matrix; the audit only establishes what happened and feeds that rule.

cases.prompt: Define how our humans and agents should work together end to end — the stages from planning to merge and closeout, who holds authority at each stage, and which gate each stage must pass.
cases.reason: Designing the process is ai-sdlc-operating-model; the governance audit consumes that process as its baseline and judges one change's compliance against it.
